conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the eligibility rules to ensure I can assess your situation accurately.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for reaching out. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with the first question: **Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. His name is Alex and he's 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

To continue assessing your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't actually. I live abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Living abroad can affect eligibility, but there are some circumstances where you may still be able to claim Child Benefit.

Could you please tell me **which country you're currently living in?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

In some cases, people living in certain countries may still be eligible for Child Benefit, particularly if they are Crown servants (such as diplomats or members of HM Forces serving abroad) or in specific circumstances related to the EU Settlement Scheme.

Could you tell me: **Are you a Crown servant (for example, working for the British government abroad, in the armed forces, or as a diplomat)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

One more question about your situation: **Do you have settled status or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me ask one final question to ensure I have a complete picture of your situation:

**Are you still paying National Insurance contributions in the UK, or do you have any ongoing connection to the UK such as working for a UK employer while living in Spain?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have any of those connections. I'm not paying National Insurance in the UK or working for a UK employer.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've provided, let me explain the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

I'm sorry, but based on your circumstances, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

The main requirement for Child Benefit is that you must live in the UK. While Alex is 6 years old and meets the age requirement (under 16), you are currently living in Spain and do not meet the UK residence requirement.

There are some exceptions that allow people living abroad to claim Child Benefit, such as:
- Being a Crown servant (diplomat, armed forces member, etc.)
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme with certain conditions met

However, you've confirmed that none of these exceptions apply to your situation.

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if you move back to the UK - you would be able to apply for Child Benefit at that time. If you have any questions or if your situation changes, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for further guidance.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Alex is 6 years old, so he meets the age requirement. However, the claimant is living in Spain and does not live in the UK. The claimant is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and has no ongoing UK connections such as paying National Insurance or working for a UK employer. Without meeting any of the exceptions that would allow someone living abroad to claim Child Benefit, the claimant does not meet the residence requirement and is therefore not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex (age 6), but is currently living in Spain. The fundamental eligibility requirement for Child Benefit is that you normally must live in the UK. While there are exceptions for Crown servants and those with certain EU Settlement Scheme statuses, the claimant does not meet any of these exceptions. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
